text: Handling deadlines effectively is a crucial skill for both work and study. Good time management starts with breaking a large task into smaller, manageable steps. Prioritization helps you decide which tasks are most important or urgent, ensuring you focus your energy where it matters most. Scope management means knowing your limits and not taking on more than you can handle, as overcommitting often leads to stress and missed deadlines. Finally, communication is key—if you foresee a problem, let your team or supervisor know early. By combining these strategies, you can meet deadlines more confidently and reduce last-minute panic.
